name: Trial-00095 Loss-Control Intrabar Validation
edge_rationale: The prior exit-surface diagnostic suggested trial-00095 benefits from clipping losses near -1R while preserving winner tails. This milestone validates whether that effect survives conservative 15m intrabar replay on frozen trial-00095 entries using original entry/stop geometry.
counterparty_or_market_mechanism: Sweep/reclaim winners may need uncapped continuation, while failed reclaims may invalidate quickly. A tighter loss-side control could reduce left-tail damage without changing the entry edge if adverse movement to the predeclared threshold occurs before the original baseline close.
frozen_assumptions: No production, PAPER, LIVE, runtime, core, execution, orchestrator, or settings changes.
Entries are frozen from trial-00095 exact parameter replay before variants are applied.
R is computed from original entry_price and original stop_loss, not from realized pnl_r.
Variants only add hard loss-side thresholds and do not cap winners.
If a loss-control threshold is touched on a post-entry 15m candle before original baseline close, the variant exits there.
The entry candle itself is excluded because BacktestRunner opens positions after close checks for that snapshot.
If threshold is not touched, original baseline pnl_r and exit reason are preserved.
No entry filters, signal thresholds, governance, risk eligibility, TP ladder, or winner duration changes.
Same-candle recovery is not credited after loss threshold touch; loss control is adverse-first.
At most a future exit-research validation verdict is allowed; no promotion-ready verdict.
expected_observation: A useful result would show that a broad loss-control band around -1R improves paired ER, PF, drawdown, left-tail losses, and cost-stressed ER across chronological folds while preserving the frozen entry count and winner tail.

kill_criteria.entry_count_mismatch: Any variant that changes the frozen entry population is invalid.
kill_criteria.replay_mismatch: If baseline replay does not match the prior diagnostic artifact count, the milestone is inconclusive until resolved.
kill_criteria.winner_cap: Any variant that caps winners without a prior loss-threshold touch is invalid.
kill_criteria.methodology_bug: Any R calculation from realized pnl_r, post-hoc threshold rescue, favorable intrabar ordering, or silent missing-candle drop invalidates the run.
kill_criteria.deployment_claim: Any promotion-ready or runtime deployment claim is invalid for this milestone.

failure_modes: The distribution clipping effect was caused by realized-R accounting and is not executable on 15m OHLC.
Hard loss thresholds trigger too often before eventual winners and reduce expectancy.
The original baseline stop is already the executable -1R boundary, leaving no meaningful loss-control improvement.
Results depend on baseline replay mismatch rather than exit behavior.
Improvement concentrates in one half-year fold or one large saved loser.
out_of_scope: Changing runtime, PAPER, LIVE, core, orchestrator, risk, or execution behavior.
Adding trailing stops, breakeven moves, time stops, TP changes, partial-exit changes, or winner caps.
author: Codex
